We need to build mines to get income. Credit mines cost 2C each, give +3C/turn. Uranium mines cost 2C, give +1U/turn. Central uranium mine costs 3C, gives +1U/turn.
We have 5C. We can build two credit mines (2C each = 4C) and have 1C left, or one credit mine and one uranium mine (2+2=4C). Since we need uranium for bomb eventually, but early game we need credits to produce units. Let's build credit mines on both credit deposits (1,6) and (5,6). That will give +6C/turn starting next turn (since mines under construction produce next turn). We'll have 5-4=1C left.
We also need to scout enemy base. We have no units. We can produce a drone (2C) for scouting. But we have only 1C left after building two credit mines. Could produce a drone first turn (2C) and build one credit mine (2C) = 4C, leaving 1C. Or produce drone and build one credit mine and one uranium mine? That's 2+2+2=6C >5. So we need to prioritize.
Let's consider: we have 3 actions per turn. We can do up to 3 actions. We can build two mines (2 actions) and produce a drone (1 action) = 3 actions. But we need credits: building two credit mines costs 4C, producing drone costs 2C = 6C total, but we only have 5C. So we cannot do all three. We could build one credit mine (2C) and produce a drone (2C) = 4C, leaving 1C. Then next turn we'll have +3C from mine +1 passive = 4C more, total 5C. Then we can build second credit mine and maybe a uranium mine.
Alternatively, build two credit mines (4C) and wait (no produce). Then next turn we have +6C +1 passive = 7C, plus 1 leftover = 8C. Then we can produce drone and maybe more.

>  I'm assessing my position as Player 2 with a base at (11,3) on turn 4, currently holding 5 credits and 2 uranium. >

I should scout the enemy base location to the west with my drone while simultaneously building up my economy with more credit mines. The enemy has a drone in my territory at (9,3), so I need to be strategic about my movements and positioning. The enemy drone is just reconnaissance and not an immediate threat since my units can't hit air anyway. For the long game, I need to think about uranium accumulation—with my current mine producing 1U per turn, I'd have enough to reach 25U by turn 80, but the deposit only lasts 14 turns before respawning, so I'll need to rebuild. Grabbing the central uranium deposit at (6,5) would double my production rate, and since I can build remotely as long as the cell is in my drone's field of view, that's worth considering. For this turn, I'll prioritize building the credit mine at (7,0) while my drone can still see it, which costs 2C and leaves me with 3C after.
> 
> Now I'm weighing whether to move the drone west to scout the enemy base or produce a second drone for more reconnaissance coverage. The credit mine is somewhat exposed near the central passages—an enemy tank could potentially push through (6,1) and threaten it—but the economy boost is significant enough to justify the risk early on.
> 
> I'm realizing the central uranium deposit at (6,5) is actually within my drone's current detection range, so I could build a uranium mine there right now for 3C instead. That would double my uranium income, which is crucial for nuclear development. The real tension is whether to scout the enemy base now or keep my drone guarding the central uranium mine while it's under construction. Scouting reveals their position and is low-cost, but I have time before nuclear becomes relevant, whereas losing the central mine to an unseen tank would be costly. Moving the drone to around (4,3) could split the difference—far enough west to detect the enemy base at (1,3) while still maintaining reasonable coverage of the center.
> 
> I'm confident in the plan: build both mines while the drone is still at (7,2) to satisfy range requirements, then move it to (4,3) to scout. The risk of both buildings being under construction simultaneously is acceptable since the enemy is likely still in economy mode at turn 4. I'll commit to this and keep the message neutral.
